title: Allow Merging with a Single Metric Selection
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to merge a single new metric recommendation into an existing dictionary metric, so that I can consolidate related metrics without needing to select multiple new recommendations first.
description:
Previously, the merge function required selecting at least 2 new metric recommendations before the merge button became active. Since users can now merge a new metric into an existing dictionary entry, the validation should be reduced to require only 1 selection.

The merge popup adapts its display based on the selection count:
- **1 metric selected**: Only shows the 'merge to existing dictionary' option (hides the create-and-merge section)
- **2+ metrics selected**: Shows both create-and-merge and merge-to-existing options
acceptance_criteria: Merge button activates when 1 metric is selected (previously required 2)
With 1 metric selected, only the merge-to-existing option is shown
With 2+ metrics selected, both create-and-merge and merge-to-existing options are shown
Merge button remains grayed out when nothing is selected
ux_changes: Merge button now activates with a single selection. The popup dynamically shows/hides sections based on selection count — single selection shows only merge-to-existing, multiple selection shows both options.
middleware_changes: Updated validation logic to accept single-metric merge operations.
implementation_notes: The popup window adapts its layout based on selection count. When only one metric is selected, the 'create and merge' section is hidden since it requires two records to designate a primary.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the adaptive popup and conditional display. With one metric selected, only the merge-to-existing option appears. Ryan approved.
